report.executive_summary:
TITLE CLEARANCE STATUS: BLOCKED FROM CLOSING

The title commitment for 675 Bunker Hill Road, Columbia, MS (Commitment #01-26006219-01T) is currently unable to proceed to closing due to a critical deed recording defect. A warranty deed recorded on 01/13/2026 names Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson as grantees instead of the intended buyer, Kyla B. Fortenberry. This failure to satisfy Requirement 4 prevents issuance of the owner's policy and must be corrected immediately through a new recorded deed. Additionally, duplicate mineral exceptions and an unresolved vested owner/seller discrepancy require immediate attention. Current readiness score: 67/100.

LENDER IMPACT: The loan policy cannot be issued until the owner's policy defect is resolved. Society Mortgage's security interest cannot be properly documented until title is cleared to the correct buyer.

report.requirements:
OUTSTANDING REQUIREMENTS (6 Total):

1. CRITICAL - Record Corrected Deed (Requirement 4): A new warranty deed must be recorded from Garner Tree Farm, LLC to Kyla B. Fortenberry. The deed recorded 01/13/2026 (Instrument #20260174) incorrectly names Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson as grantees. This must be corrected before any policy can issue. STATUS: OPEN - BLOCKING CLOSING

2. CRITICAL - Resolve Vested Owner/Seller Discrepancy (Requirement 1): Clarify the relationship between Garner Tree Farm, LLC (vested owner) and Terri G. Crawley (listed seller). Provide documentation explaining authority and circumstances. STATUS: OPEN

3. HIGH - Consolidate Mineral Exceptions (Requirements 1 & 8): Exceptions 1 and 8 contain overlapping mineral reservation language. The recorded deed confirms minerals are severed ("LESS AND EXCEPT all oil, gas, and other minerals"). Consolidate exceptions to eliminate ambiguity. STATUS: OPEN

4. MEDIUM - Submit LLC Compliance Documentation (Requirement 6): Provide (a) operating agreement and amendments or certification of none; (b) evidence of good standing; (c) resolution signed by all members authorizing the transaction; (d) any additional requirements following document review. STATUS: OPEN

5. MEDIUM - Verify Open Deed of Trust (Requirement 2): Confirm no open deed of trust exists in Marion County records through escrow inquiry. STATUS: OPEN

6. STANDARD - Record Deed of Trust (Requirement 5): Record deed of trust from Kyla B. Fortenberry to Society Mortgage securing $80,808.00. STATUS: PENDING (Cannot proceed until Requirement 4 satisfied)
report.exceptions:
STANDARD EXCEPTIONS (Will remain in final policy):
- Exception 3: Survey exceptions (standard)
- Exception 4: Rights of parties in possession (standard)
- Exception 5: Mechanics' and contractors' liens (standard)
- Exception 7: Taxes and special assessments (standard)

EASEMENT EXCEPTIONS (Acceptable - already in place):
- Exception 9: VYVX, Inc. easement (03/31/1997, recorded 06/09/1997, Book 1224, Page 57)
- Exception 10: Bunker Hill Water Association easement (08/19/2009, recorded 07/21/2010, Instrument #201002482)
- Exception 11: Bunker Hill Water Association easement (04/06/2023, recorded 04/10/2023, Instrument #20230965)

PROBLEMATIC EXCEPTIONS (Require resolution):
- Exception 1 & 8 (DUPLICATE): Both address mineral interests. Exception 1 covers "any prior reservation or conveyance of minerals of every kind and character, including oil, gas, sand and gravel." Exception 8 covers "oil, gas, coal and other mineral interests." Warranty deed confirms minerals are severed. These overlapping exceptions must be consolidated into a single, clear mineral reservation exception before policy issuance.

STANDARD CARVE-OUTS:
- Exception 2: Defects appearing after commitment date
- Exception 6: Changes in title after commitment date
- Exception 12: Taxes and assessments (2025 taxes paid; no delinquencies)
report.risk_flags:
CRITICAL RISK - BLOCKS CLOSING:
[CRITICAL] Wrong Grantee in Recorded Deed (Requirement 4 Failure): The warranty deed recorded 01/13/2026 (Instrument #20260174) conveys property to Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson instead of Kyla B. Fortenberry. This is a complete failure to satisfy Requirement 4 and creates a title defect preventing issuance of the owner's policy. A new recorded deed from Garner Tree Farm, LLC to Kyla B. Fortenberry must be obtained and recorded immediately. Without this correction, closing cannot proceed and the loan policy cannot be issued.

HIGH RISK - REQUIRES CLARIFICATION:
[HIGH] Duplicate and Overlapping Mineral Exceptions: Exceptions 1 and 8 both address mineral interests with overlapping language, creating ambiguity about actual mineral rights status. The recorded deed confirms minerals are severed ("LESS AND EXCEPT all oil, gas, and other minerals"). These exceptions must be consolidated into a single, clear exception before policy issuance to eliminate title ambiguity.

MEDIUM RISK - MUST RESOLVE:
[MEDIUM] Vested Owner vs. Seller Discrepancy: Garner Tree Farm, LLC is the vested owner, but Terri G. Crawley is listed as the seller. This fundamental discrepancy must be resolved with documentation explaining the authority and circumstances. Terri Garner Crawley is identified as an LLC member, which may explain the relationship, but formal clarification is required.

[MEDIUM] Missing LLC Compliance Documentation: No operating agreement, good standing certificate, or member authorization resolutions have been submitted. These are required before the LLC can execute binding conveyance documents. All four members (Ricky Lane McPhail, Terri Garner Crawley, Patsy Garner Brewton, Karen Garner Delancey) must authorize the transaction.
report.readiness:
READINESS SCORE: 67/100 - NOT READY TO CLOSE

CLOSING BLOCKERS (Must resolve before closing):
1. Corrected deed from Garner Tree Farm, LLC to Kyla B. Fortenberry (CRITICAL)
2. Resolution of vested owner/seller discrepancy (CRITICAL)
3. Consolidation of mineral exceptions (HIGH)
4. Submission of complete LLC compliance documentation (MEDIUM)

ITEMS SUBSTANTIALLY COMPLETE:
✓ Property identification and legal description verified
✓ Bankruptcy searches completed (no proceedings found)
✓ Tax status verified (2025 taxes paid; no delinquencies)
✓ Easement exceptions identified and acceptable
✓ Loan and owner's policy amounts established ($80,808 and $80,000 respectively)
✓ Title underwriter and commitment issued

ITEMS PENDING REQUIREMENT SATISFACTION:
- Deed of trust recording (cannot proceed until owner's title cleared)
- Final title policy issuance (blocked by deed defect)

TIMELINE IMPACT: The critical deed defect will delay closing by a minimum of 7-10 business days to obtain, execute, and record a corrected deed. Additional delays likely pending LLC documentation submission and mineral exception clarification.
report.recommendations:
IMMEDIATE ACTIONS REQUIRED (Next 24-48 Hours):

1. URGENT - Contact Seller/Grantor: Immediately contact Garner Tree Farm, LLC and Terri G. Crawley to advise of the deed recording error. Determine how Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson became grantees instead of Kyla B. Fortenberry. Obtain explanation and authorization to record corrected deed.

2. URGENT - Obtain Corrected Deed: Prepare and execute a new warranty deed from Garner Tree Farm, LLC to Kyla B. Fortenberry conveying the same property with identical legal description and mineral reservation language. Record immediately in Marion County.

3. URGENT - Clarify Vested Owner/Seller Relationship: Obtain written explanation of the relationship between Garner Tree Farm, LLC (vested owner) and Terri G. Crawley (listed seller). Provide documentation confirming Terri Garner Crawley's authority as LLC member to authorize the transaction.

4. HIGH PRIORITY - Submit LLC Documentation: Request from Garner Tree Farm, LLC:
   - Copy of operating agreement and all amendments (or certification that none exist)
   - Certificate of good standing from Mississippi Secretary of State
   - Resolution signed by all four members (Ricky Lane McPhail, Terri Garner Crawley, Patsy Garner Brewton, Karen Garner Delancey) authorizing the sale to Kyla B. Fortenberry
   - Any additional organizational documents required by title underwriter

5. HIGH PRIORITY - Consolidate Mineral Exceptions: Work with title underwriter to consolidate Exceptions 1 and 8 into a single mineral reservation exception reflecting that "all oil, gas, coal, and other minerals in, on, and under the property are reserved and excepted from this conveyance."

SECONDARY ACTIONS (After Critical Items Resolved):

6. Verify Open Deed of Trust: Confirm through escrow that no open deed of trust exists in Marion County records against the property.

7. Record Deed of Trust: Once owner's title is cleared to Kyla B. Fortenberry, immediately record the deed of trust from Kyla B. Fortenberry to Society Mortgage securing $80,808.00.

8. Final Title Review: Upon receipt of corrected deed and LLC documentation, request updated title commitment reflecting all corrections and consolidated exceptions.

LENDER COMMUNICATION:
Notify Society Mortgage immediately of the title defect and estimated 7-10 day delay to closing. Provide weekly status updates on deed correction and documentation submission. Confirm loan commitment remains valid pending title clearance.
